metadata.title: Breeder profile web research - July 15 company rollups
metadata.notes: Source-backed company-level research added after breeder affiliation review. Historical employer rights are not reassigned to a breeder's current company.

profiles.ownershipType: private family-owned breeding and nursery company
profiles.ownershipSummary: Israeli family business led across the Yossef and Ido Ben-Dor generations; public sources do not provide a complete current cap table.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Yossef Ben-Dor is the senior breeder and Ido Ben-Dor is publicly identified with management, providing visible family continuity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority family-company target. Verify the cap table, succession plan, cultivar-level title, foreign licenses, and financial scale.

profiles.ownershipType: private owner-led fruit breeding and nursery company
profiles.ownershipSummary: German commercial breeding and nursery platform publicly associated with founder and breeder Michael Neumuller; a complete ownership schedule was not found.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Michael Neumuller is the visible scientific and commercial leader, creating meaningful key-person and succession concentration.
profiles.webResearchNotes: High-priority owner-led target. Verify legal entities, partner interests, successor depth, portfolio title, and operating scale.

profiles.ownershipSummary: Miyoshi Agri-Tech is part of the broader Miyoshi horticulture group and works with public institutions including NARO on strawberry genetics.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Institutional corporate breeding bench; no founder or sole-breeder succession signal was found.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Strategic corporate and institutional relationship profile, not a small standalone acquisition candidate.

profiles.ownershipSummary: UCOLIVO is the University of Cordoba olive breeding program; protected material should be treated as university or program IP rather than individual breeder property.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Multi-researcher public program with institutional licensing and succession continuity.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public licensing and relationship profile; exclude from acquisition ranking.

profiles.ownershipSummary: CREA is a public national research body; variety rights may involve CREA institutes and commercialization partners but are not standalone breeder-owned assets.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Institutional multi-program research bench with public governance.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public licensing and partnership profile; resolve title by institute and cultivar when needed.

profiles.ownershipSummary: NARO is Japan's national agricultural R&D agency and administers or holds rights in numerous fruit cultivars.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Large institutional breeding organization with formal licensing channels and broad succession depth.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public licensing and collaboration profile; exclude from acquisition ranking.

profiles.ownershipType: private grower consortium collaborating with public research institute
profiles.ownershipSummary: ITUM is a private grower-owned consortium commercializing table-grape varieties developed with Murcia's public IMIDA program.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Consortium and public-program structure reduces key-person risk but may limit change-of-control feasibility.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Licensing and partnership target. Verify member ownership, transfer restrictions, exclusive territories, and IMIDA economics.

profiles.ownershipType: public university breeding and licensing program
profiles.ownershipSummary: Oregon State University administers the hazelnut program and related cultivar licensing; individual researchers should not be treated as owners.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Long-running university program with institutional continuity beyond any one named breeder.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Public licensing relationship, not an acquisition target.

profiles.ownershipType: private founder-led propagation and breeding company
profiles.ownershipSummary: James A. Heilig founded and operates Huron Plant Technologies. His earlier Driscoll's inventions remain attributed to Driscoll's and are not rolled into Huron.
profiles.leadershipSummary: Founder-dependent early-stage specialist with no visible successor or broad internal breeding bench.
profiles.webResearchNotes: Potential founder-led target, but the current owned-IP schedule, cap table, customer contracts, and historical assignment boundary require primary diligence.
